title: nothopanax arboreus
intro: Nothopanax arboreus, also known as the tree nettle or puawhananga in Maori, is a plant species native to New Zealand. It is a climbing plant with stinging hairs, which contain a compound called acridine, giving it its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Nothopanax arboreus include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Herbal preparations of Nothopanax arboreus can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used for their health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing.
